title: Warmly Charred Malaysian Turmeric Tofu Satay
description: A plant-based version of the traditional Malaysian dish, featuring charred and flaky tofu infused with turmeric and other spices, served with a side of peanut sauce for a nostalgic and savory experience.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together turmeric powder, coconut milk, peanut oil, peanut butter, lime juice, galangal, and salt to make the marinade.
Add the tofu skewers to the marinade and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the tofu from the marinade and cook on the grill for 3-4 minutes per side, or until charred and flaky.
Serve the satay hot with a side of peanut sauce for dipping.
To make the peanut sauce, combine peanut butter, coconut milk, and lime juice in a blender and blend until smooth.
Adjust the seasoning of the peanut sauce as needed.
Garnish the satay with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
